failures.name: File Descriptor Exhaustion
failures.how_to_induce: Open connections without closing them until EMFILE error
failures.production_frequency: weekly at scale, instantly during viral events
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — easy to miss if you only watch CPU/memory
failures.blast_radius: total service unavailability for new connections

failures.name: Disk Full During Write
failures.how_to_induce: Fill filesystem to capacity, then attempt writes
failures.production_frequency: monthly (log rotation, temp files, data growth)
failures.detection_difficulty: low — but consequences depend on write handling
failures.blast_radius: data corruption if writes are not atomic
failures.silent_or_loud: can be silent — partial writes may look valid
failures.time_to_detect_without_monitoring: hours to days (data corruption discovered later)

failures.name: CPU Saturation Cascade
failures.how_to_induce: Send compute-heavy requests while maintaining connection queue
symptoms: all requests slow (not just heavy ones)
failures.production_frequency: during traffic spikes or algorithmic inefficiency
failures.detection_difficulty: low — CPU metrics visible
failures.blast_radius: all requests affected due to shared compute

failures.name: Instance Death Mid-Request
failures.how_to_induce: kill -9 an instance while requests are in-flight
failures.production_frequency: during deploys, instance recycling, hardware failure
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — one instance of many
failures.blast_radius: requests to that instance only (if health check removes it)
failures.silent_or_loud: loud for affected requests, silent for system overall

failures.name: Slow Instance (Gray Failure)
failures.how_to_induce: Add artificial latency to one instance (sleep/CPU hog)
symptoms: connection pool to slow instance fills
failures.production_frequency: common — GC pauses, noisy neighbors, disk issues
failures.detection_difficulty: high — instance passes health checks but is slow
failures.blast_radius: fraction of requests get bad latency, averaged out in P50
failures.silent_or_loud: silent to operators, loud to affected users
failures.time_to_detect_without_monitoring: days (shows up in customer churn, not dashboards)

failures.name: Thundering Herd on State Store
failures.how_to_induce: Restart all instances simultaneously
failures.production_frequency: during bad deploys, mass restart, failover events
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — state store metrics spike
failures.blast_radius: total system unavailability until instances stabilize

failures.name: Crash Without Write-Ahead Log
failures.how_to_induce: kill -9 during write operation (no WAL)
failures.production_frequency: any unexpected process death or power loss
failures.detection_difficulty: varies — may only surface as data inconsistency later
failures.blast_radius: all writes since last successful flush are lost
failures.silent_or_loud: silent — no error at read time, just missing/corrupt data

failures.name: WAL File Corruption
failures.how_to_induce: Manually corrupt bytes in WAL file, then restart
failures.production_frequency: rare — disk errors, filesystem bugs
failures.detection_difficulty: detected at startup if checksums exist
failures.blast_radius: all uncommitted entries in corrupted WAL segment
failures.silent_or_loud: loud at recovery time — crash loop or data loss

failures.name: Concurrent Writers + Crash
failures.how_to_induce: Multiple writers + kill -9 at random point
failures.production_frequency: any multi-instance setup writing to shared storage
failures.detection_difficulty: high — may appear as 'correct' data that's actually stale
failures.blast_radius: subset of concurrent operations lost
failures.silent_or_loud: silent — no error, just missing data

failures.name: Cold Cache Stampede
failures.how_to_induce: Flush/restart cache while serving production traffic
failures.production_frequency: every cache restart, every deploy that clears cache
failures.detection_difficulty: low — very visible
failures.blast_radius: entire system — database is the shared dependency

failures.name: Stale Data Window
failures.how_to_induce: Update source data, immediately read from cache
failures.production_frequency: continuous — every write has a staleness window
failures.detection_difficulty: high — no errors, data looks valid, it's just old
failures.blast_radius: user trust erosion, business logic errors

failures.name: Hot Key + TTL Expiry
failures.how_to_induce: Single key accessed by many clients + let TTL expire
symptoms: N simultaneous cache misses for same key
failures.production_frequency: any popular content with TTL expiry
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — looks like a traffic spike on one query
failures.blast_radius: database load spike, potentially cascading

failures.name: Consumer Death Mid-Processing
failures.how_to_induce: kill -9 consumer after dequeue but before ack
failures.production_frequency: every deploy, every instance restart
failures.detection_difficulty: depends on delivery guarantee implementation
failures.blast_radius: one message affected per crash

failures.name: Producer Flood / Consumer Backpressure
failures.how_to_induce: Produce messages 10x faster than consumer capacity
failures.production_frequency: traffic spikes, batch imports, upstream bursts
failures.detection_difficulty: low — queue depth is visible
failures.blast_radius: processing latency for all messages in queue

failures.name: Poison Message (Infinite Failure)
failures.how_to_induce: Enqueue message that causes processing exception every time
failures.production_frequency: any time input validation is incomplete
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — consumer appears busy but queue isn't draining
failures.blast_radius: blocks all subsequent messages in ordered queues

failures.name: Leader Node Failure
failures.how_to_induce: kill -9 the leader process
failures.production_frequency: hardware failures, network events, deploys
failures.detection_difficulty: low — writes immediately fail
failures.blast_radius: all writes blocked until failover completes

failures.name: Replication Lag Stale Reads
failures.how_to_induce: Add artificial delay to replication, read from follower
failures.production_frequency: continuous under write-heavy load
failures.detection_difficulty: high — no errors, just stale data
failures.blast_radius: user trust, business logic errors from stale state

failures.name: Split-Brain Data Divergence
failures.how_to_induce: Network partition, write to both leader and follower
failures.production_frequency: rare but catastrophic — network partitions
failures.detection_difficulty: high — only discovered when partition heals
failures.blast_radius: permanent data corruption if not detected

failures.name: Hot Partition (Skewed Workload)
failures.how_to_induce: Send 80% of requests to keys on one shard
symptoms: one shard saturated while others idle
failures.production_frequency: any power-law distribution (celebrities, viral content)
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — per-shard metrics reveal it
failures.blast_radius: all data on that shard affected

failures.name: Shard Unavailability
failures.how_to_induce: Kill one shard node entirely
symptoms: requests for that shard's keys fail
failures.production_frequency: hardware failures, network isolation
failures.detection_difficulty: low — clear error for specific key ranges
failures.blast_radius: 1/N of data unavailable (N = number of shards)

failures.name: Rebalancing During Traffic
failures.how_to_induce: Add new shard and trigger data migration while serving traffic
failures.production_frequency: every scale-up event
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — elevated error rate during migration
failures.blast_radius: all migrating keys temporarily affected

failures.name: Minority Node After Partition
failures.how_to_induce: Partition 3-node cluster into 2+1
symptoms: must step down as leader if it was one
failures.production_frequency: network events, switch failures
failures.detection_difficulty: low — consensus protocol rejects operations
failures.blast_radius: minority side becomes read-only or unavailable

failures.name: Split-Brain Dual Leader
failures.how_to_induce: Symmetric partition where each node is isolated
failures.production_frequency: rare — requires specific partition topology
failures.detection_difficulty: very high — each side thinks it's operating correctly
failures.blast_radius: catastrophic — conflicting data requiring manual resolution

failures.name: Stale Lock Holder After Pause
failures.how_to_induce: Hold a lock, pause the process (SIGSTOP), lock expires, another acquires, resume original
symptoms: two processes believe they hold the lock
failures.production_frequency: GC pauses, process scheduling delays
failures.detection_difficulty: very high — requires fencing token validation
failures.blast_radius: data corruption from concurrent unsafe access

failures.name: Saga Step Failure After Prior Commits
failures.how_to_induce: Fail service C after services A and B have committed
symptoms: A and B committed, C failed
system in inconsistent state until compensation
failures.production_frequency: any multi-service transaction under load
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — requires saga state tracking
failures.blast_radius: one transaction in inconsistent state

failures.name: Synchronous Timeout Cascade
failures.how_to_induce: Make one downstream service slow (30s response)
failures.production_frequency: any slow dependency in synchronous call chain
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — looks like everyone is slow (masks root cause)
failures.blast_radius: entire call chain, all requests through that path

failures.name: Compensation Step Failure
failures.how_to_induce: Trigger compensation (refund API) and have it fail too
symptoms: customer sees inconsistent state for extended period
failures.production_frequency: when failure conditions affect multiple services
failures.detection_difficulty: high — requires saga state monitoring
failures.blast_radius: stuck transactions accumulate, manual work grows

failures.name: Single-Service Latency Injection
failures.how_to_induce: Add 2s delay to one downstream service
symptoms: P99 latency spikes in calling service
failures.production_frequency: database slow queries, GC pauses, dependency issues
failures.detection_difficulty: depends entirely on observability quality
failures.blast_radius: all paths through that service

failures.name: Low-Rate Intermittent Errors
failures.how_to_induce: Fail 1% of requests randomly in one service
failures.production_frequency: memory leaks, race conditions, flaky dependencies
failures.detection_difficulty: high — too rare for individual traces, requires statistical alerting
failures.blast_radius: 1% of affected path's users

failures.name: Cascading Latency (Multi-Service)
failures.how_to_induce: Slow service C, observe A→B→C latency propagation
symptoms: root cause hidden by downstream symptoms
failures.production_frequency: any shared dependency slowdown
failures.detection_difficulty: high without distributed tracing — medium with it
failures.blast_radius: all paths through the affected dependency

failures.name: Thread Pool Exhaustion from Slow Dependency
failures.how_to_induce: Make one dependency slow without circuit breaker, send traffic
symptoms: ALL endpoints become slow (not just the one calling slow dependency)
failures.production_frequency: any unprotected slow dependency
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — symptom appears in the CALLER, not the root cause
failures.blast_radius: entire calling service (all endpoints)

failures.name: Retry Storm After Brief Outage
failures.how_to_induce: Brief outage (5s), clients retry immediately with no backoff
failures.production_frequency: every brief outage without proper retry policies
failures.detection_difficulty: low — traffic spike immediately visible
failures.blast_radius: recovering service gets hit again, extends outage

failures.name: Circuit Breaker Extending Outage Duration
failures.how_to_induce: 5s dependency failure, circuit breaker timeout set to 60s
symptoms: dependency recovered in 5s but circuit stays open for 55 more seconds
failures.production_frequency: misconfigured circuit breakers — very common
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — circuit breaker state visible if instrumented
failures.blast_radius: all requests to that dependency fail for the open duration

failures.name: 10x Traffic Validates/Disproves Estimate
failures.how_to_induce: Generate 10x normal traffic load
symptoms: first bottleneck reveals itself — often NOT what was predicted
failures.production_frequency: viral events, partnership launches, sales events
failures.detection_difficulty: low — very visible when it hits
failures.blast_radius: depends on which component breaks first

failures.name: Gradual Resource Leak Under Sustained Load
failures.how_to_induce: Sustained moderate load for hours (connections not closed, memory not freed)
symptoms: works fine for hours then suddenly fails
failures.production_frequency: memory leaks, connection leaks, file handle leaks
failures.detection_difficulty: high — not visible in short tests, only under sustained load
failures.blast_radius: eventual total failure after long stable period

failures.name: Weakest Component Cascading at Scale
failures.how_to_induce: Scale traffic until the component with lowest ceiling breaks, observe cascade
failures.production_frequency: every growth phase that outpaces one component
failures.detection_difficulty: medium — capacity metrics reveal it, but may not have alerts
failures.blast_radius: system-wide once the weakest link is saturated
